name: csvagg
description: A standard-library-only CSV parser and aggregator. Parse(r) reads CSV via encoding/csv treating the first record as a header, and GroupSum groups rows by a key column and sums the integers in a value column into a map[string]int, with explicit errors for missing columns, short rows, and non-integer values.

maintain.request: Add a GroupMean feature: aggregate the average integer value per group, returning map[string]float64, plus an underlying GroupStats method that exposes both Sum and Count per group. It must reuse the existing parse and validation pipeline and share the same error semantics as GroupSum (missing columns, short rows, non-integer values). Refactor GroupSum so the short-row and value-parsing logic is shared rather than duplicated.

review.bug: In GroupSum the accumulator is declared as `var sums map[string]int` (a nil map) instead of `make(map[string]int)`, so the first `sums[key] += v` write panics with "assignment to entry in nil map" for any input that has at least one data row.
keywords: assignment to entry in nil map
